LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER AMARITA KING'S PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS GRANTED. THE AUGUST 26, 2016 DECISION OF HEARING OFFICER J. DENNY IS VACATED AND THE CASE REMANDED TO THE ANIMAL CARE AND CONTROL DEPARTMENT TO DETERMINE WHETHER OR NOT TO HOLD ANOTHER VICIOUS AND DANGEROUS DOG HEARING REGARDING BAM BAM. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS.) WRITTEN ORDER AND STATEMENT OF DECISION IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. A JUDGMENT IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. calendar.matters:
First Amended Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus, Changing Hearing Date From 9/6/2017 To 9/22/2017
Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Text Ruling

Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kathleen L. Moran of the law offices of Kathleen L. Moran (415-994-1342) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party, Amarita King

Deputy City Attorney, Margaret W. Baumgartner of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3859) appears as counsel for respondent/non-opposing party, San Francisco County

On Petitioner's request, Court makes clarification regarding tentative ruling as set forth in the record. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER AMARITA KING's Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is GRANTED. The August 26, 2016 decision of Hearing Officer J. Denny is vacated and the case remanded to the Animal Care and Control Department to determine whether or not to hold another Vicious and Dangerous Dog Hearing regarding Bam Bam. If the Department chooses to do so, the Department must select a new hearing officer or, if J. Denny is the hearing officer, in advance of the hearing J. Denny must disclose all he can recall regarding communications he has had about Bam Bam that did not take place at either the May 12, 2016 or August 18, 2016 hearings and give interested persons an opportunity to respond at the hearing to what was stated in those communications. The August 26, 2016 decision must be vacated for two reasons. First, page 3 of that decision is clearly a non sequitur and leaves substantial doubt whether any order was made regarding Bam Bam and, if so, the precise terms of that order. Although it is highly likely that the third page was appended to the statement of decision by mistake, the parties and the public should not be required to speculate about what terms were intended. Second, Mr. Denny's participation in undisclosed ex parte communications regarding Bam Bam deprived Ms. King of a fair hearing. While Ms. King may no longer have been the owner of Bam Bam, she remained vitally interested in any orders concerning Bam Bam and should be afforded the right to challenge the August 26, 2016 statement of decision. Written order and statement of decicion is signed and filed in open court. A judgment is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No: 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Sep-22-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PETITIONER AMARITA KING's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is continued to September 22, 2017 per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
